items.title: Free dental clinic changes lives in cost-of-living crisis

children.content: Demand is high for an Adelaide dental program that offers free services to patients who have experienced homelessness or who have trouble accessing conventional care.

items.title: Woman who appeared 'belligerent' after fatal crash sentenced

children.content: A woman who was found to be mentally incompetent when she caused a fatal multi-car crash has been released into a transitional mental health facility.

image.altText: A woman with long dark hair wearing a black tshirt sits in a room at a desk. A green chair is behind her

items.title: Concerns about light and noise from $1b outback energy project resolved

children.content: The battery project, which will provide back-up power for up to 80,000 homes in Broken Hill, will go ahead with new conditions after a dispute was resolved between the company and an outback stargazing business.

image.altText: Aerial view of highway with a few residences pictured on the ground.

items.title: MP's extra voting paper for SA upper house among electoral problems under scrutiny

children.content: As authorities commit to a review of problems faced by South Australian voters during this month's election, a former Liberal MP says he was concerned to received an extra ballot paper in the post.

articles.title: One Nation claims seat of MacKillop, its third in South Australia's lower house

children.content: A former immigration detention system worker on Christmas Island has won the seat of MacKillop.

image.altText: A headshot of a man smiling at the camera.

gallery.altText: A collection of SA ballot papers including two for the Legislative Council.

thumbnail.altText: A collection of SA ballot papers including two for the Legislative Council.

items.title: Businesses report increase in abuse from customers as fuel crisis worsens

children.content: From pile-on comments on social media to roadside slurs, local businesses are reminding their communities to "be kind to the little guy" amid the fuel crisis.

image.altText: a woman stares at the camera with a houseboat in the background

items.title: Coroner finds paramedic who made 'man flu' comments failed to meet standards

children.content: A coroner has recommended the South Australian Ambulance Service consider implementing an inclusive language program after an inquest heard an Indian national was told he was overreacting with the "man flu" in the days before he died of chronic pneumonia.

image.altText: Paramedic in green work clothes with grey hair stands next to ambulance

items.title: Artists and scientists unite to throw spotlight on seaweed's 'beauty'

children.content: South Australia's rugged south-east coast is known for its cold water smashing into limestone cliffs. But beneath the surface is a global hotspot of seaweed biodiversity.

image.altText: An underwater shot of colourful seaweed.

items.title: South Australia wins back-to-back Shield titles with stunning win over Victoria

children.content: South Australia has won back-to-back Sheffield Shield titles with a brilliant comeback victory over Victoria in the final at Melbourne's Junction Oval.

image.altText: South Australia bowler Henry Thornton jumps, shouts and punches the air to celebrate a Sheffield Shield wicket.

items.title: SA rules out free public transport, for now, as dozens of servos are out of some fuels

children.content: The South Australian government has no plans to follow other states in offering free public transport despite more than 70 service stations running out of at least one fuel type.

image.altText: A tram at a level crossing with a bus waiting behind boomgates

items.title: 36ers lodge complaint to NBL over Bogut's 'personal attacks' on owner

children.content: Drama from the spiteful championship series between the Adelaide 36ers and Sydney Kings goes off the court as the 36ers complain to the NBL about the conduct of Kings co-owner and assistant Andrew Bogut.

image.altText: Andrew Bogut points with his left hand

items.title: Two shootings, 150km apart: Dezi Freeman's final destination mystifies locals

children.content: The fugitive resurfaced more than 150 kilometres away from the town where he shot and killed two police officers seven months ago. Mystified locals are questioning how.

articles.title: Freeman joins a long line of fugitives captured or killed in Australia

children.content: Australia has a long history of violent outlaws heading bush to avoid capture. But how is it still possible to remain on the run for so long?

image.altText: A photo of Dezi and an image of Ned Kelly

items.title: 'Evil inside him': Dezi Freeman's nephew speaks out on cop killer uncle's death

children.content: When Dezi Freeman was shot dead it left his family with mixed emotions. Some are shattered, others know an "evil man" is dead. His nephew speaks out.

image.altText: Two men smiling for a photo in a backyard.

items.title: Blake would be 29 this year if not for a whooping cough outbreak

children.content: The latest immunisation data shows more than 80,000 Australian children aged five and under are not fully up to date with their vaccinations.

items.title: Anger at developer's plan to build $10m mansion on illegally cleared land

children.content: A property developer who illegally cut down hundreds of native trees has lodged a new plan to build a lavish $10 million, six-bedroom mansion in Sydney's south.

items.title: Japan deploys first long-range missiles capable of reaching mainland China

children.content: The upgraded Type-12 missile has a range of about 1000 km, allowing it to now reach mainland China.

image.altText: The Type-12 land-to-ship missile launcher is shown at the Camp Kengun in the Kumamoto prefecture

items.title: Remote Australians angered by Vodafone ad claiming 'nothing's out here'

children.content: A new Vodafone campaign has regional Australians seething after the telecommunications giant claimed to focus its network infrastructure on where "98 whatever per cent of you actually live".

items.title: Calls for tourism lease of world heritage area island to be cancelled

items.title: Travel quiz: Get out your passport and see if you can beat our quiz

children.content: Which is Australia’s oldest Luna Park? And where would you find Elvis Presley’s mansion?

items.title: Is E10 fuel bad for my car? And could it save me money?

children.content: Australia's leading motoring organisation is urging drivers to fill up with E10 fuel in an effort to conserve national fuel supplies. But what exactly is E10? And could it help ease the current fuel crisis?

items.title: The Drama's surprising twist is almost guaranteed to ignite controversy

items.title: Family of Australian aid worker killed by IDF still waiting for answers

children.content: Three women share their experiences of never having a partner — not by choice — and what that's like when you wish you did.

items.title: Transphobia nearly killed Kaz. Now he just wants to be 'me'

children.content: This WA teenager was depressed, ill and missing out on school. He says it was part of the toll transphobia took on him.

items.title: Fair Work Commission abolishes junior pay rates for young adults

children.content: The Fair Work Commission has issued its decision to abolish junior pay rates for young adult employees in the retail, fast food, and pharmacy sectors while maintaining them for minors.

items.title: Prized WA tourism hotspots forced to close to holiday-makers for Easter

children.content: Famed holiday hotspots in Western Australia's north, including Exmouth and Coral Bay, are reluctantly turning away tourists ahead of the Easter holiday peak as they rebuild in the wake of Cyclone Narelle.

articles.title: Early government modelling shows how long fuel stockpile lasts if imports slow

children.content: Diesel stocks would last about six months if Australia's imports dropped by 20 per cent, according to modelling compiled for the federal government early in the Middle East war.

items.title: Report card issued on Antarctic division's cultural reforms

children.content: Workplace reforms implemented by the Australian Antarctic Division (AAD) after two damning reports into the organisation's culture have led to some improvements but a review has found problems persist.

items.title: Jackie O claiming 'at least' $82m in legal action against ARN Media

items.title: IOC's proposed gender test not fit for purpose, says man who discovered it

children.content: The scientist who discovered the SRY gene test that the International Olympic Committee is now relying on to determine athletes' eligibility for women's events says it should not be used in such a fashion.

items.title: Vaping likely to cause cancer, new Australian review of evidence finds

children.content: Nicotine-based vapes are likely to cause lung and oral cancer, according to a new review of evidence. Experts say there needs to be tougher enforcement of vape laws in Australia.
